danger_signals.visual_form: Animated hostile sprites (Hot Heads, Waddle Dees, Gordos, Cannons)
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate threat requiring evasion or absorption

danger_signals.visual_form: Visible damage counter that decreases on contact
danger_signals.meaning: Cumulative danger state; loss of all health = game over

danger_signals.visual_form: Screen-bottom abyss, visible gaps in platforms
danger_signals.meaning: Instant death if Kirby falls; vertical level design emphasizes vulnerability

danger_signals.visual_form: Door leading to enclosed boss chamber
Temporary power-up icon disappears from UI
danger_signals.meaning: Return to vulnerable state; window of danger reopens

safety_signals.visual_form: Animated sprite effect surrounding Kirby; icon displayed in HUD
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary immunity to damage; safe to engage enemies

safety_signals.visual_form: Icon displayed in ability HUD; visual transformation of Kirby (e.g., Fireball adds hat, Sword adds blade)
safety_signals.meaning: Enhanced combat capability; expanded level traversal options

safety_signals.visual_form: Distinct door graphics within world lobbies
safety_signals.meaning: Safe retreat to previous worlds; checkpoint/navigation structure

safety_signals.visual_form: Indoor room tileset distinct from outdoor levels

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Kirby depicted in various power-up forms (Fireball Kirby with crown, Sword Kirby with blade, UFO Kirby with disc, Wheel Kirby with spinning form)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Presence in dream-themed worlds; association with Fountain of Dreams restoration

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Varied enemy designs serving as Dedede's allies; minibosses include Poppy Bros. Sr., Mr. Frosty, Rolling Turtle
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Enemies appear in different power-up states (some grant copy abilities when consumed)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Scattered throughout seven worlds; miniboss arenas contain themed encounters; Star Rod pieces distributed among allies

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Fountain of Dreams corruption; spatial separation (outer space final battle)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Depicted as corrupting force; final boss battle distinct from miniboss encounters
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Associated with fountain's corruption; manifests in cutscene triggering endgame sequence

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Residents depend on Fountain of Dreams for restful sleep
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Implied presence through narrative context; Kirby's motivation to restore Star Rod stems from protecting them

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Fountain of Dreams corrupted by Nightmare
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Intro cutscene depicting corruption; fountain inaccessible at game start
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: King Dedede steals Star Rod and distributes pieces; minions occupy worlds; Kirby must travel across seven worlds to retrieve fragments

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Visual progression through world lobbies; completed worlds may show altered state
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Boss defeats result in piece acquisition; Warp Star doors enable backtracking

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Final cutscene showing Kirby using Star Rod's power against Nightmare in outer space
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Endgame sequence; transition from terrestrial Dream Land to cosmic confrontation

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Kirby's sprite transforms based on copy ability; HUD icon shows current power
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Different attack patterns, movement speeds, and level-traversal capabilities available per power-up

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Health meter visual representation; possibly visual cues on Kirby sprite when low
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Increased vulnerability; single hit may trigger game over; player behavior shifts toward caution

environmental_storytelling.example: Each of seven worlds contains 4-5 regular levels, boss arena, miniboss arena, museum, and minigame room—organizing narrative progression through spatial design
Vegetable Valley uses agricultural graphics; later worlds employ different visual themes to signal progression through Dream Land geography
Different enemy distributions per level communicate environmental hazards; presence of specific minibosses signals world boss arenas
Guaranteed ability acquisition rooms provide safety zones separate from combat; signal player resource management opportunities
Opening cutscene depicts corruption; final confrontation in outer space represents escalation from terrestrial to cosmic stakes
Crane game and other minigames provide narrative breaks and reward mechanics; spatial distinction from main levels signals optional challenge content

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Normal walking/running speed; can inhale, spit, float, run, slide; base vulnerability to all damage
Enhanced attack patterns; different movement properties; sprite visually altered (crown appears); ability persists until damage or voluntary discard
Immune to damage; can safely contact enemies; temporary state requiring re-acquisition after loss
Animated movement patterns; some enemies fast/slow (e.g., 'extremely fast Waddle Dee' exists in unused form); attack on player proximity
Advanced attack patterns (ice cube throws); arena-confined behavior; defeat triggers copy ability grant and boss door unlock
Outer space confrontation; requires Star Rod power to defeat; conclusive endgame encounter
Door selection available; choice paralysis mitigated by transparent progression structure (numbered doors, distinct room types)

notes: Kirby's Adventure employs clear visual hierarchy to communicate world state: protagonist color (pink) provides immediate identification; power-up visual transformations create readable mechanical differentiation; enemy sprite variety telegraphs encounter difficulty; UI elements (health meter, ability icon, world map progression) support state communication without diegetic interruption. The seven-world structure with internal room organization (4-5 levels + boss + miniboss + museum + minigame) creates predictable spatial communication patterns that guide player navigation and expectations. Environmental storytelling relies heavily on tileset theming and enemy placement rather than narrative text. Copy abilities function as both mechanical and visual communicators—transforming Kirby's sprite while expanding gameplay options, signaling both power progression and narrative advancement through puzzle-solving capability gates.
